User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the OmniVid AI application, authenticated via Google OAuth. This entity stores basic profile information and timestamps related to user activity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

googleId.description: The unique identifier provided by Google for this user via Google OAuth 2.0.

email.description: The user's email address, obtained from Google. Used for communication and identification.

displayName.description: The user's display name or full name, obtained from Google.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, obtained from Google.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was first created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the user's most recent login time.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a generated video within the OmniVid AI application, including details about its creation, status, and associated user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated this video. (Relationship: User 1:N Video)

originalPrompt.description: The original text prompt provided by the user for video generation.

promptLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 code for the language of the original prompt (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'es').

optimizedPrompt.description: The AI-optimized, 'AI-friendly' English version of the prompt used for generation.

visualStyle.description: The chosen visual style for the video (e.g., 'Cinematic', 'Anime', 'Realistic').

voiceoverLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 code for the language chosen for the AI voiceover.

videoUrl.description: The URL to the generated video file, stored in Google Cloud Storage.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL to a thumbnail image for the generated video.

status.description: The current generation status of the video (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed', 'blocked').

resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video (e.g., '720p', '1080p').

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the video in seconds.

isAdSupported.description: Indicates if the video generation was initiated through the ad-supported queue for 'Fast Pass' generation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation request was made.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation process was completed.

watermarkApplied.description: Indicates if an invisible digital watermark (SynthID) has been applied to the video.

safetyScanResult.description: The result of the content safety scan performed by Google Cloud Video Intelligence API (e.g., 'clean', 'blocked', 'review_required').

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Documents are primarily accessed and managed by the authenticated user whose UID matches the `{userId}` wildcard. This collection supports secure private user data storage.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Contains video documents generated by a specific user. Each video document includes a denormalized `userId` field (matching the parent path's `userId`) for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the authenticated user who owns the video.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who generated the video, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific generated video.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles. It establishes clear ownership and access patterns for user data and generated videos.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This structure achieves authorization independence by explicitly embedding the `userId` within the `Video` document itself (`/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`). This denormalization means that any security rule for a `Video` document does not need to perform a `get()` operation on its parent `User` document to ascertain ownership or permissions. Rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with both the `userId` wildcard in the path (`{userId}`) and the `resource.data.userId` field within the `Video` document. This avoids complex, non-atomic rule evaluations and ensures that document creation (even in batches or transactions) can be securely verified without external reads.

**QAPs (Query as a Security Principle):**
1.  **`/users/{userId}`:** Access to this collection is strictly path-based. An authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can only read/write their own user profile document at `/users/{request.auth.uid}`. This inherently supports QAPs, as any query to this collection will naturally be filtered by the authenticated user's UID, ensuring they can only access their own profile.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`:** For `Video` documents, the hierarchical path combined with the denormalized `userId` field ensures robust QAP support. When a user requests their list of videos (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/videos`), the security rules will only permit reads if the `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid` AND the `resource.data.userId` within each video document matches the `userId` in the path. This guarantees that `list` operations automatically enforce ownership, preventing users from inadvertently or maliciously querying videos belonging to other users. The collection's homogeneous security posture (all documents owned by the parent user) further simplifies rules and improves list query security and efficiency.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   User profiles are segregated into `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that each user's profile is a private, path-owned document.
*   Videos are segregated into a subcollection `/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`, representing a clear `User 1:N Video` relationship. This path-based ownership is the most secure and debuggable pattern for user-owned data.
*   The `Video` entity's `status` field (`pending`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`, `blocked`) uses explicit state modeling, enhancing clarity and simplifying rule logic.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Semantic and descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{videoId}`) are used, and authorization fields (like `userId`) are standardized and consistently named across the schema and implicitly by the path structure. This promotes predictability and ease of understanding for both developers and security rule auditors.
